name: Grenada

colors.symbolism: courage, vitality, and the desire for independence
warmth, wisdom, the sun, and the friendliness of the people
the lush vegetation, agriculture and fertility of the islands

description: A red rectangular border surrounds an inner field divided by yellow diagonals into two yellow triangles (top and bottom) and two green triangles (hoist and fly). A central red disc bears a yellow five-pointed star; six more yellow stars sit on the red border (three top, three bottom). A small yellow nutmeg pod is placed on the green triangle at the hoist.
history: Adopted on 7 February 1974, the day Grenada gained independence from the United Kingdom. The flag was designed by Anthony C. George.

features.symbolism: a red rectangular border encloses an inner field divided per-saltire into two yellow triangles (top and bottom) and two green triangles (hoist and fly); a red disc with a yellow star sits at the centre, six smaller yellow stars sit on the red border, and a yellow nutmeg pod is placed on the hoist green triangle
background for the central star, repeating the red of the border

features.role: six smaller stars on the red border (three on the top edge, three on the bottom)
features.symbolism: the six original parishes of Grenada

features.role: nutmeg pod on the inner field at the hoist
features.symbolism: Grenada's nickname, the Isle of Spice, and its leading export, nutmeg
